pr.title: More matches in ftSk_SpecialLw and ftYs_SpecialN
pr.author: Gelatart

summary: Matched several previously stubbed or undecompiled functions in Sheik down-B transform and Yoshi neutral-B/special state code. The PR filled in two ftSk_SpecialLw accessory/effect callbacks, implemented Yoshi's aerial neutral-B entry, a Yoshi transition/helper function, and a small Yoshi helper that calls common cliff/ground routines. It also tightened two ftYs_SpecialN.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void signatures and added an efSync_Spawn note documenting the variadic call shape needed for one Sheik effect spawn.
what_changed: Implemented fn_80114034 in ftSk_SpecialLw.c: obtains Fighter via GET_FIGHTER, spawns effect 0x4FC on fp->parts[FtPart_R2ndNb].joint when fp->x2219_b0 is false, sets x2219_b0 true, installs efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll as hitlag callbacks, and clears accessory4_cb.
Implemented fn_801140B0 in ftSk_SpecialLw.c with the same callback pattern as fn_80114034, but spawning effect 0x4FD on fp->parts[FtPart_HipN].joint.
Added ef/eflib.h and ef/efsync.h includes to ftSk_SpecialLw.c to support effect spawning and hitlag callback assignments.
Changed ftSk_SpecialLw_Enter from an undecompiled marker comment into an empty function body, indicating it matched as an empty function in this slice.

Implemented ftYs_SpecialAirN_Enter in ftYs_SpecialN.c: resets fp->cmd_vars[0], changes motion state to 0x15F, calls ftAnim_8006EBA4, and registers callbacks through ftCommon_8007E2D0 using fn_8012D0A0, fn_8012D004, and ftCo_800BBB8C.

Implemented fn_8012D004 in ftYs_SpecialN.c: calls it_802F2BFC with fp->x1A60 and gobj, changes motion state to 0x161 with flags 0x81092 and current animation frame, sets fp->x2222_b2 and fp->mv.ys.specialn.x0.x0_bytes.x0_b0, then calls ftYs_SpecialS_8012DF00, ftCommon_8007E2F4, and ftCommon_8007E2FC.
Implemented fn_8012EFC0 in ftYs_SpecialN.c as a small wrapper that calls ft_80089824 and ft_800892A0.
Updated ftYs_SpecialN.h prototypes for fn_8012D004 and fn_8012EFC0 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete void signatures taking HSD_GObj* and ftYs_GObj* respectively.
Added a comment to efSync_Spawn's header noting that fn_80114034 appears to want a variadic call like efSync_Spawn(0x4FC, gobj, fp->parts[FtPart_R2ndNb].joint).
smart_moves: Reused the common Sheik effect-callback idiom: guard the one-time spawn with fp->x2219_b0, attach the effect to a specific Fighter part joint, then set pause/resume callbacks for hitlag behavior.

Kept effect spawning through efSync_Spawn's existing variadic declaration rather than forcing a premature typed overload; the added header note records the observed call shape without overcommitting the global API.
Converted Yoshi header declarations from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS only where the implementation evidence provided concrete signatures, improving type information incrementally.
Used existing common fighter helpers such as Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftAnim_8006EBA4, ftCommon_8007E2D0, ftCommon_8007E2F4, and ftCommon_8007E2FC rather than open-coding state setup.
Preserved function-address-style names such as fn_8012D004 where no semantic name is evidenced, avoiding speculative renaming.
decomp_lessons: For accessory/effect callbacks, check for a fighter boolean latch before spawning effects; in this PR x2219_b0 prevents duplicate efSync_Spawn calls.
When matching fighter effect spawns, the exact FtPart joint matters: Sheik's two callbacks use FtPart_R2ndNb and FtPart_HipN with different effect IDs.
Hitlag behavior for spawned effects can require assigning fp->pre_hitlag_cb to efLib_PauseAll and fp->post_hitlag_cb to efLib_ResumeAll inside the accessory callback.
Small wrapper functions can be legitimate matches; fn_8012EFC0 only calls ft_80089824 and ft_800892A0.
Empty functions can also be matches; ftSk_SpecialLw_Enter was changed from a decomp marker to an empty function body.
Header cleanup should follow implementation progress: replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete prototypes once a function body establishes argument and return types.
PR body notes that some work was incomplete due to decomp.me server issues, so absence of nearby matches should not be interpreted as a design choice.
naming_conventions: Character special files use prefixes matching the fighter and move, e.g. ftSk_SpecialLw for Sheik down-B and ftYs_SpecialN for Yoshi neutral-B.
Unidentified functions retain address-based names such as fn_80114034, fn_801140B0, fn_8012D004, and fn_8012EFC0.
Concrete entry points use action-oriented names such as ftYs_SpecialAirN_Enter and ftSk_SpecialLw_Enter.
Fighter part enum names such as FtPart_R2ndNb and FtPart_HipN identify attachment joints for effects.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Match variadic efSync_Spawn calls by passing the effect ID, HSD_GObj*, and the exact joint pointer from fp->parts[...].joint.
For motion-state transitions, preserve numeric state IDs and flags observed in the target: ftYs_SpecialAirN_Enter uses state 0x15F with flags 0, while fn_8012D004 uses state 0x161 with flags 0x81092.
Use explicit casts where callback or helper signatures do not yet align cleanly, as shown by casts to Fighter_GObj*, Fighter*, and callback function pointer types in Yoshi neutral-B code.
State-machine matching may require setting command variables and nested move-state bitfields before calling common transition helpers.
The evidence suggests that exact callback assignment order and clearing accessory4_cb were part of the Sheik effect callback match.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the supplied PR slice.

The PR body states that some functions were not completed yet due to decomp.me server issues and that ftSk_SpecialLw was close on the last two remaining functions, but those remaining functions were not included here.
searchable_terms: More matches in ftSk_SpecialLw and ftYs_SpecialN
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs completing the remaining ftSk_SpecialLw functions mentioned in this PR body.
Search for other efSync_Spawn variadic call sites that attach effects to fp->parts[...].joint and use x2219_b0 guards.
Search for subsequent cleanup of fn_8012D0A0, fn_8012EDE8, fn_8012EFF4, and other remaining UNK_RET declarations in ftYs_SpecialN.h.
Check whether efSync_Spawn eventually received stronger typed wrappers or documentation beyond the comment added here.
